goal: Land the daemon-side .ucil/ state primitives needed by the rest of Week-3: (a) a `storage` module that initialises the two-tier `.ucil/shared/` + `.ucil/branches/<branch>/` layout per master-plan §11.2 (P1-W2-F06) and (b) a `Checkpoint` helper in the existing `lifecycle` module that persists last-indexed progress to `.ucil/checkpoint.json` and restores it on restart (P1-W3-F09). Both frozen selectors — `-p ucil-daemon storage::test_two_tier_layout` and `-p ucil-daemon lifecycle::test_crash_recovery` — require tests living at MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests { }`). The WO-0006 rejection trio (`0c604df`, `ab943e5`, `a8ab537`) all cited the same `storage::test_two_tier_layout` selector mismatch caused by a `mod tests { }` wrapper — DEC-0005's commit-size waiver did not repair the selector mismatch, only the critic complaint. This WO starts fresh from `main` (where no `storage.rs` exists today) and MUST place the two frozen-selector tests at the flat module root, mirroring the existing `lifecycle::test_pid_file_*` precedent at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs:257-333`. Unblocks P1-W3-F02 (file watcher, depends on F06) and, transitively, P1-W3-F03 (Watchman integration).
plan_summary: Create `crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs` with a `StorageLayout` type whose `init(base, branch)` creates `.ucil/shared/`, `.ucil/branches/<sanitized-branch>/`, `.ucil/sessions/`, `.ucil/plugins/`, `.ucil/backups/`, `.ucil/otel/`, `.ucil/logs/` via `std::fs::create_dir_all` (idempotent). Path-accessor methods expose the four subpaths called out in master-plan §11.2 lines 1066-1088 (`shared_knowledge_db_path`, `branch_symbols_db_path`, `branch_vectors_dir`, `branch_tags_lmdb_path`). Branch names containing `/` are sanitised to `-` so filesystem layout is flat. Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs` with a `Checkpoint { last_indexed_commit: Option<String>, active_branch: String, saved_at: u64, daemon_version: String }` struct plus `Checkpoint::write(path, &checkpoint)`, `Checkpoint::read(path) -> Result<Option<Checkpoint>>`, and `Checkpoint::restore_or_new(path, default_branch) -> Result<Checkpoint>`. Write path goes through `OpenOptions::create().truncate().write()` + `serde_json::to_writer_pretty` + `flush()` — no atomic-rename dance (§11.2 checkpoint.json is single-writer, daemon-owned). Read path returns `Ok(None)` when the file does not exist (fresh start) and `Err(CheckpointError::Parse(_))` only when the file exists but JSON is malformed. `restore_or_new` is the thin convenience wrapper: `read()?` and if `None`, return a freshly-constructed `Checkpoint` with `last_indexed_commit = None`, `active_branch = default_branch`, `saved_at = now()`, `daemon_version = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")`. Tests live at MODULE ROOT (flat `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_<name>()` pattern — see `lifecycle.rs:257-333` for the exemplar), NOT inside `mod tests { }`. Frozen selectors: `storage::test_two_tier_layout` (F06) and `lifecycle::test_crash_recovery` (F09) — the selector-mismatch rejection mode that took down WO-0006 three times MUST NOT recur.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs — NEW FILE. Module rustdoc cites master-plan §11.2 lines 1060-1089 as the layout spec and §18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1737 as the feature bullet. Declares: `pub struct StorageLayout { base: PathBuf, branch: String }` (fields `pub(crate)` on the struct; constructors return a fully-initialised value). Derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`. Fields are `base: PathBuf` (absolute path to the `.ucil/` root, i.e. `<repo>/.ucil`) and `branch: String` (sanitised branch name — slashes replaced with `-`)

crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs — `pub enum StorageError` with `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive]` and variants: `#[error("io at {path}: {source}", path = path.display())] Io { path: PathBuf, #[source] source: std::io::Error }`, `#[error("empty branch name")] EmptyBranch`. Private constructor for Io should match the pattern at `lifecycle.rs:56-63` (named `{path, source}` struct variant with `#[source]` on source)

crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs — `impl StorageLayout { pub fn init(base: &Path, branch: &str) -> Result<Self, StorageError> }` — creates the seven directories (`shared`, `branches/<sanitized-branch>`, `sessions`, `plugins`, `backups`, `otel`, `logs`) via `std::fs::create_dir_all` so re-running is idempotent. Empty `branch` returns `StorageError::EmptyBranch`. `/` → `-` sanitisation BEFORE any fs call. `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", fields(branch = branch))]` per master-plan §15.2 (span name `ucil.daemon.storage.init`)

crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs — `impl StorageLayout { pub fn base(&self) -> &Path; pub fn branch(&self) -> &str; pub fn shared_dir(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn branch_dir(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn sessions_dir(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn plugins_dir(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn logs_dir(&self) -> PathBuf }` — read-only path accessors computed from `self.base` + `self.branch`. All `#[must_use]`

crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs — `impl StorageLayout { pub fn shared_knowledge_db_path(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn shared_memory_db_path(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn shared_history_db_path(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn branch_symbols_db_path(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn branch_vectors_dir(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn branch_tags_lmdb_path(&self) -> PathBuf; pub fn branch_state_json_path(&self) -> PathBuf }` — file-level helpers matching §11.2 lines 1066-1076 verbatim (`shared/knowledge.db`, `shared/memory.db`, `shared/history.db`, `branches/<branch>/symbols.db`, `branches/<branch>/vectors/`, `branches/<branch>/tags.lmdb`, `branches/<branch>/state.json`). All `#[must_use]`

crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs — MODULE-ROOT tests (flat `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` — NOT inside `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0005 + the WO-0006 rejection post-mortem). Required flat tests (exact names — `test_two_tier_layout` is the FROZEN F06 SELECTOR):

* `test_two_tier_layout` — FROZEN F06 SELECTOR. Creates a `tempfile::TempDir`, calls `StorageLayout::init(tmp.path(), "main")`, asserts every directory exists: `shared/`, `branches/main/`, `sessions/`, `plugins/`, `backups/`, `otel/`, `logs/`. Then re-runs `init` on the same path and asserts no error (idempotency). Asserts the accessor paths (`shared_knowledge_db_path`, `branch_symbols_db_path`, etc.) resolve under the expected subtrees

* `test_branch_name_with_slashes_is_sanitised` — calls `init(tmp, "feat/new-thing")`; asserts `branches/feat-new-thing/` exists; asserts `StorageLayout::branch()` returns `"feat-new-thing"`; asserts `branches/feat/` does NOT exist
* `test_empty_branch_rejected` — calls `init(tmp, "")`; asserts `StorageError::EmptyBranch`
* `test_init_is_idempotent` — runs `init(tmp, "main")` three times in a row; asserts no error each time; asserts the final directory set matches the first run

* `test_shared_paths_are_branch_independent` — constructs two `StorageLayout`s with the same `base` and different branches; asserts `shared_knowledge_db_path` returns the same path for both; asserts `branch_symbols_db_path` differs

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — EXTEND existing file. Add `pub struct Checkpoint { pub last_indexed_commit: Option<String>, pub active_branch: String, pub saved_at: u64, pub daemon_version: String }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]`. Module rustdoc at the top of `lifecycle.rs` (lines 1-17) gets a third bullet cross-referencing the new Checkpoint role (next to PidFile + wait_for_shutdown)

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — `pub enum CheckpointError` with `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive]` and variants: `#[error("checkpoint io at {path}: {source}", path = path.display())] Io { path: PathBuf, #[source] source: std::io::Error }`, `#[error("checkpoint parse at {path}: {source}", path = path.display())] Parse { path: PathBuf, #[source] source: serde_json::Error }`. Co-located with existing `PidFileError` so the file keeps a single Errors section

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — `impl Checkpoint { pub fn write(path: &Path, value: &Checkpoint) -> Result<(), CheckpointError> }` — creates/truncates the file with `OpenOptions` (same pattern as `PidFile::write` at `lifecycle.rs:113-136`), writes `serde_json::to_writer_pretty`, flushes. No atomic-rename dance (single-writer daemon-owned file). `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(value))]` per §15.2 (span name `ucil.daemon.lifecycle.checkpoint.write`)

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — `impl Checkpoint { pub fn read(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Checkpoint>, CheckpointError> }` — returns `Ok(None)` when the file does not exist (fresh start / pre-first-run), returns `Err(CheckpointError::Parse{..})` when the file exists but JSON is malformed. Reads via `std::fs::read_to_string` + `serde_json::from_str`. `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug")]` (span name `ucil.daemon.lifecycle.checkpoint.read`)

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — `impl Checkpoint { pub fn restore_or_new(path: &Path, default_branch: &str) -> Result<Checkpoint, CheckpointError> }` — convenience wrapper: `Self::read(path)?.unwrap_or_else(|| Checkpoint::new(default_branch.to_string()))`. Pairs with `Checkpoint::new(active_branch: String) -> Self` (private or `pub`; prefer `pub` for construction-in-tests) which sets `last_indexed_commit = None`, `active_branch = active_branch`, `saved_at = now_unix_secs()`, `daemon_version = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").to_string()`

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — private `fn now_unix_secs() -> u64` helper (same shape as `session_manager.rs:54-59`) used by `Checkpoint::new`. If a shared version is preferred, promote the `session_manager::now_unix_secs` to `pub(crate)` — but the simpler duplication follows the precedent at `session_ttl.rs` (same function). Choose duplication for this WO to keep blast radius minimal
crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — MODULE-ROOT tests (flat `#[cfg(test)] #[test]`). Required flat tests (exact names — `test_crash_recovery` is the FROZEN F09 SELECTOR):

* `test_crash_recovery` — FROZEN F09 SELECTOR. Creates a `tempfile::TempDir`, constructs a `Checkpoint { last_indexed_commit: Some("abc123def".into()), active_branch: "main".into(), saved_at: 1712345678, daemon_version: "0.1.0".into() }`, writes it via `Checkpoint::write(&path, &cp)`, simulates a restart by calling `Checkpoint::restore_or_new(&path, "fallback")`, asserts `last_indexed_commit == Some("abc123def")`, asserts `active_branch == "main"` (NOT the `fallback`), asserts `saved_at == 1712345678`. Also asserts that deleting the file and calling `restore_or_new(&path, "dev")` returns a fresh checkpoint with `last_indexed_commit == None`, `active_branch == "dev"`, `daemon_version == env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")`
* `test_checkpoint_write_then_read_roundtrip` — writes a fully-populated `Checkpoint` and reads it back; asserts all four fields round-trip via serde exactly
* `test_checkpoint_read_missing_returns_none` — calls `Checkpoint::read(tmp.path().join("nope.json"))`; asserts `Ok(None)` (not an error)
* `test_checkpoint_read_malformed_returns_parse_error` — writes `"not json"` bytes to a file; asserts `Checkpoint::read(&path)` returns `Err(CheckpointError::Parse { .. })`

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — extend the existing re-exports. Add `pub use storage::{StorageError, StorageLayout};` to the re-export block. Extend `pub use lifecycle::{Lifecycle, PidFile, PidFileError, ShutdownReason};` to include `Checkpoint, CheckpointError` — so the final line reads `pub use lifecycle::{Checkpoint, CheckpointError, Lifecycle, PidFile, PidFileError, ShutdownReason};` (alphabetical). Extend the lib.rs module-level rustdoc (lines 1-16) with a short bullet for `storage` + `Checkpoint`

Every new public item gets a rustdoc block. Error types get `# Errors` sections. `init` gets a `# Examples` block using `tempfile::tempdir()` (mark `no_run` since it touches the filesystem — actually keep it as a runnable doctest if it compiles; the `tempfile` crate is already a `dev-dependencies` entry but doctests can bring it in via `#[cfg(doc)]` — simpler to use `no_run` and cite the test as the canonical example)

rejection-commits:0c604df, ab943e5, a8ab537 (all three are WO-0006 verifier rejections citing `storage::test_two_tier_layout` selector mismatch caused by `mod tests { }` wrapper — THIS is the rejection mode to avoid)

work-order:ucil-build/work-orders/0006-symbol-extraction-chunker-storage.json (historical context — do NOT reuse its branch; do NOT cherry-pick its aa8c4aa commit; start fresh from main)

impl-precedent:crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs:257-333 (flat module-root test pattern — 7 tests shipped green in WO-0021; `#[cfg(test)]` + `#[test]` on a free function at module root, NOT inside `mod tests { }`)

impl-precedent:crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs:56-73 (PidFileError variant shape: `#[error("... at {path}: {source}", path = path.display())] Io { path, #[source] source }` — StorageError::Io and CheckpointError::Io follow this shape)

rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror; pedantic+nursery; tracing; no unwrap outside #[cfg(test)]; no unsafe without ADR; edition 2021)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (Conventional Commits; Feature/Phase/Work-order trailers; ~50 lines/commit soft target)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariants 1-9; F06 listed in scope; F09 listed in scope)
lessons_applied: FROZEN-SELECTOR placement (DEC-0005 post-mortem + WO-0006 rejection trio): both `storage::test_two_tier_layout` and `lifecycle::test_crash_recovery` are module-root selectors. Placing the test inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` makes the actual nextest path `<module>::tests::<test_name>` — NO match. Mirror the `lifecycle.rs:257-333` precedent (7 tests already shipped green in WO-0021) where each test is a free function decorated with `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` at module root. The acceptance_criteria grep guard on `mod\s+tests\s*\{` catches this specific regression before it reaches the verifier

WO-0006 rejection cause was solely the selector-mismatch (3× REJECT citing `storage::test_two_tier_layout` not resolving). DEC-0005 was an ADR about commit size, NOT about selector placement — the commit-size waiver does NOT save a WO that misplaces its frozen-selector tests. This WO-0022 therefore has module-root placement as a hard acceptance criterion, not a style recommendation

Session-abandonment prevention (WO-0012 → WO-0020 lesson): before ending the session verify (a) at least one `.rs` diff against `origin/main` exists on the feature branch, (b) every commit has been pushed, AND (c) `0022-ready-for-review.md` has been written. If any is missing, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the blocker

Branch discipline (WO-0020 lesson): start `feat/WO-0022-storage-layout-and-crash-recovery` fresh from `main`. Do NOT rebase or cherry-pick from `feat/WO-0006-symbol-extraction-chunker-storage` even though it has a storage.rs — that file has the wrong test placement and its tree is historical. Fresh module, fresh tests, fresh commits

Additive extension of `lifecycle.rs` (WO-0021 precedent): the `lifecycle` module already shipped with PidFile + ShutdownReason + Lifecycle in WO-0021. Adding `Checkpoint` + `CheckpointError` + `test_crash_recovery` is purely additive. Do NOT refactor existing PidFile/ShutdownReason/Lifecycle APIs. Do NOT move code into a submodule (`lifecycle/checkpoint.rs`) — the frozen F09 selector requires `lifecycle::test_crash_recovery` at module root

Idempotency on filesystem init (master-plan §10.2 and WO-0006 intent): `std::fs::create_dir_all` is already idempotent; `StorageLayout::init` re-running on the same path must succeed. The `test_init_is_idempotent` test pins this contract

Branch-name sanitisation (§11.5 + WO-0006 aa8c4aa commit-message line about `/` → `-`): branch names containing `/` (e.g. `feat/new-thing`) become directory components; sanitise to `-` BEFORE any fs call so the layout stays flat. `test_branch_name_with_slashes_is_sanitised` pins this contract

Checkpoint round-trip is the crash-recovery oracle (F09 description + §11.2): the F09 feature description reads 'daemon stores last-good checkpoint in .ucil/checkpoint.json; on restart it resumes indexing from the checkpoint without full re-index.' The `test_crash_recovery` test simulates a restart as write→read→assert-fields-identical; this IS the acceptance, not some arbitrary daemon-restart fuzz

Read-missing-is-Ok-None (not an error): first-run daemons have no checkpoint.json yet. `Checkpoint::read` must distinguish 'not found' (Ok(None)) from 'found but malformed' (Err(Parse)). `test_checkpoint_read_missing_returns_none` and `test_checkpoint_read_malformed_returns_parse_error` pin this contract

No atomic-rename dance (master-plan §11.2 is silent; single-writer daemon): `.ucil/checkpoint.json` is written by one process (the daemon owns its `.ucil/` dir). Plain `OpenOptions::create().truncate().write()` + `serde_json::to_writer_pretty` + `flush()` is sufficient. If crash-mid-write ever becomes a real concern, raise an ADR — not a pre-emptive complexity add

Clippy pedantic + nursery in effect (inherited from `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs:19`) — expect `missing_errors_doc`, `missing_const_for_fn`, `must_use_candidate`, `doc_markdown`, `similar_names`, `module_name_repetitions`. Fix, don't blanket-allow. `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` on the new `storage.rs` matches the `session_manager.rs:13` + `lifecycle.rs:37` precedent for modules whose public types name-prefix the module

Conventional Commits with trailers (rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md): every commit carries `Phase: 1`, `Work-order: WO-0022`, and the correct `Feature:` trailer — `P1-W2-F06` on storage-only commits, `P1-W3-F09` on checkpoint-only commits, and BOTH on the lib.rs re-export commit. Push after every commit. No `--amend` after push. No force-push

Reality-check / mutation-oracle survival (cf. WO-0017 / WO-0018 / WO-0019 / WO-0021): `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W2-F06` and `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W3-F09` MUST both pass. The script stashes source files changed by commits trailed `Feature: P1-W2-F06` or `Feature: P1-W3-F09`; stashing must break the selectors; popping must restore. Ensure BOTH the impl AND tests for each feature are in commits trailed with that feature — the oracle stashes the UNION of changed files across all `Feature: <id>` commits

No shared cross-feature commits beyond re-exports: the single lib.rs commit that re-exports `StorageLayout + Checkpoint` is the only commit trailed with BOTH features. Every other commit trails exactly one feature so the mutation oracle can stash cleanly per-feature

commit_plan.subject: storage: add StorageLayout + StorageError with init + path accessors + 5 module-root tests
commit_plan.note: Module-introduction commit — DEC-0005/DEC-0001 waiver applies if splitting produces non-compiling intermediates (new struct + impl + tests bound together). Tests at module root, NOT inside mod tests {}
